Urgent | US Central Command: The destruction of the tower in Chabahar weakens the ability of the Revolutionary Guard to coordinate attacks on civilian ships

RT by @hey_itsmyturn: On July 16, US forces successfully destroyed the observation tower at Shahid Kalantari port in Chabahar, which was part of the maritime surveillance system along the coast of Iran in the Gulf of Oman. For decades, the array was used by the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps to monitor and attack commercial vessels crossing the Strait of Hormuz. The destruction of the tower directly harms the IRGC's ability to coordinate attacks against innocent civilian personnel. In addition, the attack helps ensure the freedom of navigation in the waters of the region for all vessels, with the exception of vessels attempting to violate the ongoing naval blockade imposed by the United States on Iran.

The US Army: Yesterday, American forces were able to destroy the control tower at Shaheed Kalantri Port in Chah Bahar

U.S. forces destroyed the Chah Bahar Shahid Kalantari Port surveillance tower, part of an IRGC maritime surveillance network along Iran's Gulf of Oman coast used to track and target commercial vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz.

RT by @no_itsmyturn: On July 16, U.S. forces successfully destroyed the Chah Bahar Shahid Kalantari Port surveillance tower, part of a maritime surveillance network along Iran’s Gulf of Oman coastline used for decades by the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) to track and target commercial vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz. The destruction of the tower directly degrades IRGC’s ability to coordinate attacks on innocent civilian crew members. Furthermore, the strike protects freedom of navigation in regional waters for all vessels, except for ships attempting to violate the ongoing U.S. naval blockade against Iran.

RT by @lookner: Hormuz traffic reaches three week low MarineTraffic data indicate confirmed crossings through the monitored Strait of Hormuz zone fell to eight on 16 July, down from 15 a day earlier and marking a three week low. Seven of the eight transits followed the Iranian route, with no crossings via the Omani route and no shadow fleet movements recorded. Traffic was evenly split between commercial and non commercial vessels, while half of all crossings were Iranian flagged. The latest data reflect a continued decline in vessel activity, reinforcing signs of a more selective operating environment across one of the world's busiest maritime chokepoints. Read more here: https://okt.to/RMnKqW

After 47 years of terrorism, kleptocracy, and repression, the only durable solution is the regime’s complete dismantlement and President Trump has done more than any predecessor to make that possible. But one element has been missing since fighting broke out in February: the Iranian people. The regime long ago lost their consent and survives on fear alone. Uprisings are coming faster and spreading wider: from 80+ cities in 2017, to 100+ in 2019, to 160+ during Women, Life, Freedom in 2022, to over 200 cities across all 31 provinces this year, the largest revolt since 1979. The regime’s bankrupt economy manufactures new protesters every month, and even its conscripts and Basij come from the same squeezed families they’re ordered to shoot. The next uprising in Iran is a question of when, not if, and likely before Trump’s term ends. What’s missing is the third leg alongside military and economic leverage: an operational strategy to stand with Iranians when they rise. That means breaking the information blackout with Starlink and satellite connectivity, degrading the regime’s machinery of repression through cyber operations, contingency planning to protect protesters from massacre, and quietly offering regime elites and commanders a path out. No boots on the ground. No sanctions relief to the regime. Just foresight and the time to act is now.
